parameters.name: const
parameters.label: Constant
parameters.description: Sequence of name/value pairs defining the channels

parameters.name: const0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: The name of the channel.

parameters.name: const0value
parameters.label: Value
parameters.description: The value of the channel.

parameters.name: snap
parameters.label: Snapshot Input
parameters.description: The optional first input on Constant is used when the Snapshot Input button is pressed. At this time, the channel names and values at the input at the current frame are used to initialize the channel

parameters.name: first
parameters.label: First Channel
parameters.description: The First Channel parameter is used to select a smaller set of the incoming channels. This is useful if the number of incoming channels is greater than the 40 channels the Constant can hold, and you m

parameters.name: current
parameters.label: Active Needs Current
parameters.description: This is used with the second input as described above, when you want to add a displacement to channels by using external devices or sources.

parameters.name: single
parameters.label: Single Sample
parameters.description: Turn this Off to make constant channels that are longer than one .

parameters.name: start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: Start and end of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples). The parameters are expressed in the found on the Common page. To set the to be 100 samples long, Set to Samples, Off and

parameters.name: startunit
parameters.label: Start Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: end
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: Start and end of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples). The parameters are expressed in the found on the Common page. To set the to be 100 samples long, Set to Samples, Off and

parameters.name: endunit
parameters.label: End Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: The sample rate of the channels, in samples per second.

parameters.name: left
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description: The left extend conditions (before range).

parameters.name: right
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description: The right extend conditions (after range).

parameters.name: defval
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: The value used for the Default Value extend condition.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

examples.label: Set a named constant value
examples.description: Create a channel named 'speed' with value 2.5.

examples.label: Define two parameter channels
examples.code:
c = op('constant1')
c.par.name0 = 'width'
c.par.value0 = 1920
c.par.name1 = 'height'
c.par.value1 = 1080

examples.label: Read a constant value in a script
